title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: March 03, 2026: Geopolitical Tensions & Inflation Concerns Weigh on Markets

items.headline: Wall Street Navigates Geopolitical Storm Amidst Mixed Close
items.summary: U.S. equities experienced a mixed close today, March 3, 2026, following a volatile trading session marked by heightened geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and persistent inflation concerns. While technology and energy sectors showed resilience, broader market sentiment remained cautious, with major indices seeing a mixed performance by the end of the day. Early trading saw significant declines across the board, only to recover partly by the close.

items.headline: Target Rises on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Target Corporation ($TGT) saw a gain of 3.3% after reporting better-than-expected profits for its latest quarter, with Q4 net sales aligning with company expectations and adjusted EPS favorable to last year.

items.headline: Airlines Decline Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: United Airlines ($UAL) fell 5%, American Airlines ($AAL) sank 4.4%, and Delta Air Lines ($DAL) dropped 4% today, as escalating geopolitical tensions and rising oil prices negatively impacted the airline sector. The conflict has also led to canceled flights and stranded passengers.

items.headline: Energy and Technology Show Strength, Consumer Staples Lagging
items.summary: Today's market saw a mixed performance across sectors. The Energy Select Sector SPDR ($XLE) advanced 1.30%, while the Technology Select Sector SPDR ($XLK) saw a 0.9% gain. Conversely, the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR ($XLP) declined 1.4%, with Communication Services ($XLC) down 1.60% and Financials ($XLF) down 1.29%. Utilities ETFs are also in demand due to the increasing energy needs of data centers.

items.headline: Inflation and Geopolitics Drive Economic Concerns
items.summary: Economic data released today included U.S. Domestic Vehicle Sales and Eurozone preliminary CPI flash estimates for February, expected to show moderating trends. However, January's producer inflation spiked to 0.8%, reigniting fears of stagflation. Federal Reserve officials, including New York Fed President John C. Williams, acknowledge solid growth but note inflation remains above the 2% target, currently around 3%. The market anticipates rate cuts later in the year, likely not before June.

items.headline: Key Companies Report Earnings, Best Buy's Sales Decline
items.summary: Several major companies released earnings today. Best Buy Co., Inc. ($BBY) reported a 0.8% decrease in comparable sales for Q4 FY26, though diluted EPS was $2.56. Progressive ($PGR) also held its Q4 2025 earnings call. On Holding AG ($ONON) announced full-year net sales surpassing CHF 3.0 billion for the first time.

items.headline: Middle East Conflict Fuels Oil Price Surge and Market Volatility
items.summary: Macroeconomic trends are heavily influenced by the escalating Middle East conflict, particularly the U.S.-Israel military actions against Iran and the potential closure of the Strait of Hormuz. This has led to a significant surge in oil prices, with Brent crude leaping 7.5% and U.S. crude rising 7.6% today. The uncertainty is driving market volatility and risk-off sentiment globally.
